meaning: n.连续,继承,继任,演替,[农业] 轮栽,连续性
examples: They are semi-finalists for the fourth year in succession.
He fired three shots in quick succession.
They had three children in quick succession.
In general, advisors should start succession planning when they hit their peak earning years.
Rangers burst to life after 17 minutes and created three good chances in quick succession.
